short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $2.41B in FY2026 Q1, +65.5% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $1.98B (-17.8% vs current Q) — set by 21 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $3.30 vs current $190.00.
Margin context: 2.7% net margin (8.4% op margin) — compressed — watch operating leverage.
Next earnings: 2026-08-19 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $7B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $8B → FY2 $9B (+12.3% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +49.7% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · trading window)
bullets: Price $494.81 vs SMA50 $450.82 (+9.8%) — short-term trend is bullish (price holding above the near-term moving average).
RSI(14): 60.4 — constructive momentum.
MACD: 14.03 vs signal 17.82 — bearish crossover.
30-day return: +3.7% — directional bias entering the next 4 weeks.
Bollinger band position: 45% of band width (band width 11.4% — normal).
Near-term S/R: resistance ~$509.65, support ~$479.97. 43% of 52w range (low $380.47 → high $645.35).
short_term.takeaway: Short-term trader bias: long (trend + momentum aligned bullish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · positioning window)
bullets: Price $494.81 vs SMA200 $472.47 (+4.7%) — long-term trend is in **uptrend intact**.
Moving average regime: Death cross active (SMA50 < SMA200) — institutional trend filter bearish.
1-year return: -4.1% — range-bound.
Drawdown from 52w high: -23.3% — correction territory
Cycle S/R: long-term resistance $645.35 (52w high), structural support $380.47 (52w low).
90-day return: +12.3% — positive intermediate-term momentum.
long_term.takeaway: Long-term positioning: wait for a trend-confirmation breakout / breakdown before sizing up.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 60.8% — elevated (premium-selling favored).
Put/Call OI: 0.51 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).
30-day implied move: ±14.9% ($421.09 – $568.53) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -8.15pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $530.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $470 (5% OTM). High IV (61%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 50.5% across 6 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 6 dates spanning 121–611 DTE.
212-day implied move: ±38.9% ($302.13 – $687.49) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $600 (+21.3% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-10.2pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Buy 1-year LEAPS call at $445 (10% ITM, 70-delta proxy). Long-cycle trend up + sell-side sees +21.3% upside to $600 PT → ITM LEAPS gives ~80% of stock exposure at ~40% of capital, with limited downside vs holding.

fundamentals: Revenue +65.5% YoY (strong for Information Technology; sector strong-bar 15%)
Net margin 2.7% (thin for Information Technology; sector strong-bar 18%)

options: P/C OI 0.51 (call-heavy) — genuine bullish positioning (trend confirms)
